title: Fever and extreme agitation in a 32-year-old female
presentation: A 32-year-old female with known Graves disease is brought in extremely agitated and confused. Her family says she stopped taking her methimazole 3 weeks ago and had a URI last week. Vitals: BP 158/72, HR 158 irregular, RR 26, Temp 40.1C, SpO2 96%. Exam: exophthalmos, diffuse goiter, atrial fibrillation on monitor, hyperreflexia, fine tremor.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Burch-Wartofsky score >45 = thyroid storm. Give PTU BEFORE iodide. Beta-blocker for rate control. Steroids block peripheral T4 to T3 conversion. Precipitant was infection.
